# open-wispr

> Free, open-source voice dictation for macOS. Powered by Whisper AI, runs 100% on-device with a proofreading pipeline for automatic punctuation, filler removal, and sentence capitalization.

open-wispr is a lightweight macOS app that provides push-to-talk voice dictation. Hold a hotkey, speak, release — transcribed text appears at your cursor in any app. It uses OpenAI's Whisper model running locally via whisper.cpp, so no audio or text ever leaves the machine.

This fork adds a **proofreading pipeline** — post-ASR correction that runs fully on-device at zero added latency: filler word removal, contraction repair, spoken-punctuation fallback, sentence capitalization, and prompt priming for continuity. Also features auto model cleanup: old models are deleted when you switch to a new one.

## Key Facts

- **Price**: Free and open source (MIT license)
- **Platform**: macOS (Apple Silicon and Intel)
- **Privacy**: 100% on-device. Zero network requests. Default maxRecordings=0: temp file + immediate delete, and startup clears any older retained recordings. Set maxRecordings 1–100 to enable Recent Recordings.
- **Install**: One-liner: `/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/totnormal/open-wispr/feat/proofreading-pipeline/scripts/remote-install.sh)"` — or clone and build from source
- **Source**: https://github.com/human37/open-wispr
- **Website**: https://open-wispr.com/

## How It Works

1. Hold the Globe key (or a custom hotkey)
2. Speak naturally
3. Release — text is typed at your cursor via macOS Accessibility

## Features

- Works in any app (text editors, browsers, Slack, Terminal, etc.)
- Multiple Whisper model sizes (tiny, base, small, medium, large)
- Configurable hotkey, language, and model via `~/.config/open-wispr/config.json`
- Runs as a background Homebrew service
- No GUI, no menubar icon, no overhead

## Configuration

```json
{
  "hotkey": { "keyCode": 63, "modifiers": [] },
  "modelSize": "base.en",
  "language": "en",
  "spokenPunctuation": false,
  "proofreadingMode": "standard",
  "maxRecordings": 0,
  "toggleMode": false
}
```

Config file location: `~/.config/open-wispr/config.json`

### Proofreading modes
- **`standard`** (default) — Full post-processing: filler word removal, contraction repair, sentence capitalization, and spoken-punctuation fallback
- **`minimal`** — Raw whisper output with only noise markers stripped

## System Requirements

- macOS 13 (Ventura) or later
- Apple Silicon (M1+) or Intel Mac
- ~150 MB disk space for the default Whisper model
- Microphone and Accessibility permissions
